title: Advanced Routing and Dynamic Peer Inspection
description: Inspect logical-router state, FIB/RIB behavior, path monitoring, and dynamic peer health across OSPF, OSPFv3, and BGP.

metadata.descriptionHtml:
This runbook inspects Palo Alto advanced routing with #show advanced-routing fib, #show advanced-routing route, #show advanced-routing logical-router, #show advanced-routing ospf, and #show advanced-routing bgp.

  1. Start with forwarding: inspect FIB and RIB state for the destination or address family in question.
  2. Confirm logical-router health: review logical-router resources and path-monitor data.
  3. Split by protocol: inspect OSPF, OSPFv3, and BGP state separately so control-plane issues are not conflated.
metadata.commandsBlock:
# Step 1: Inspect FIB and route state
> show advanced-routing fib
> show advanced-routing route destination <ip/netmask>
> show advanced-routing logical-router <logical-router-name>
> show advanced-routing resource logical-router <logical-router-name>
> show advanced-routing static-route-path-monitor

# Step 2: Inspect dynamic protocols
> show advanced-routing ospf neighbor
> show advanced-routing ospfv3 neighbor
> show advanced-routing bgp summary logical-router <logical-router-name>
> show advanced-routing bgp peer detail peer-name <peer-name> logical-router <logical-router-name>
> show advanced-routing bgp peer received-routes peer-name <peer-name> afi <ipv4|ipv6|both> logical-router <logical-router-name>
> show advanced-routing bgp peer filtered-routes peer-name <peer-name> afi <ipv4|ipv6|both> logical-router <logical-router-name>
> show advanced-routing bgp peer advertised-routes peer-name <peer-name> afi <ipv4|ipv6|both> logical-router <logical-router-name>
> show advanced-routing bgp route afi <ipv4|ipv6|both> logical-router <logical-router-name>
prerequisites: Knowledge of the logical router, peer, or destination under investigation
attackerGains: Understanding of whether the fault is forwarding, path-monitor, or dynamic control-plane related
detectionNotes: Advanced routing inspection is read-only but reveals sensitive internal topology and route-policy behavior
Administrative access to routing state should remain tightly controlled

steps.name: Inspect RIB for Target Prefix
steps.description: The operator queries advanced route state to determine which route source is selected for the affected destination.
steps.tooltip: This starts the investigation at the route-selection layer rather than at the protocol layer.

steps.name: Validate Forwarding Installation
steps.description: The firewall's selected route is translated into forwarding state inside the FIB.
steps.tooltip: FIB inspection confirms whether the route is actually installed for forwarding.

steps.name: Review Logical Router Resources
steps.description: Logical-router status and resource information are collected to determine whether the routing domain itself is healthy.
steps.cli:
> show advanced-routing logical-router <logical-router-name>
> show advanced-routing resource logical-router <logical-router-name>
steps.tooltip: Logical-router health is a separate concern from individual protocol adjacency.

steps.name: Expose Path Monitor State
steps.description: Static-route path-monitor evidence is added to the report to catch path-health issues outside protocol adjacency.
steps.tooltip: Path-monitor failure can remove otherwise valid-looking routes from service.

steps.name: Inspect OSPF Neighbor Health
steps.description: OSPF and OSPFv3 neighbor evidence is collected to verify adjacency and LSDB health.
steps.cli:
> show advanced-routing ospf neighbor
> show advanced-routing ospfv3 neighbor
steps.tooltip: This isolates dynamic control-plane issues related to OSPF family protocols.

steps.name: Inspect BGP Route Exchange
steps.description: BGP summary, peer detail, and route views reveal whether routes are being received, filtered, advertised, or dampened as expected.
steps.cli:
> show advanced-routing bgp summary logical-router <logical-router-name>
> show advanced-routing bgp peer detail peer-name <peer-name> logical-router <logical-router-name>
> show advanced-routing bgp peer received-routes peer-name <peer-name> afi <ipv4|ipv6|both> logical-router <logical-router-name>
> show advanced-routing bgp peer filtered-routes peer-name <peer-name> afi <ipv4|ipv6|both> logical-router <logical-router-name>
> show advanced-routing bgp peer advertised-routes peer-name <peer-name> afi <ipv4|ipv6|both> logical-router <logical-router-name>
> show advanced-routing bgp route afi <ipv4|ipv6|both> logical-router <logical-router-name>
steps.tooltip: This is the main control-plane branch for external route exchange and route-policy verification.

steps.name: Deliver Routing Conclusion
steps.description: The combined forwarding, logical-router, path-monitor, and protocol evidence is returned to the operator as a routing conclusion.
steps.tooltip: The operator can now decide whether the incident is caused by route installation, peer health, or route-policy behavior.
